commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c3652e632d7c6c03a7429791649bf1968787fd30)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds stable Discord reaction keys, requests a heartbeat after accepted reaction events enqueue, and updates Discord reaction tests, docs, and changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main enqueues accepted Discord reactions as system events without a heartbeat wake, and the PR proof exercises the listener path after the patch. I did not run a live long-running Discord Gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs maintainer/product direction on the default reaction wake policy and coordination with the open shared-policy PR before an automated repair lane can safely change it.

target.title: Discord: wake on reaction events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord reaction notifications were enqueued as passive system events, so reaction-only inputs could wait for a later wake before the agent processed them.
- Why it matters: Discord is a low-noise operator surface; a reaction should be enough to get the routed session's attention when reactions are already enabled and authorized.
- What changed: accepted Discord reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and request an immediate heartbeat wake after the system event is successfully enqueued.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no per-emoji semantics/config API, no hardcoded emoji-to-action map, and no new Discord permissions. Emoji meanings can live in preferences/prompt text.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Discord reaction-only events now wake the routed session after enqueue, and standard/custom emoji are exposed as stable reaction keys in the system event text/context key.
- Real environment tested: Redacted OpenClaw development checkout on this PR head, plus a live Discord API canary using the configured Discord bot and target. No secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs are included here.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  After this patch, ran the focused Discord reaction monitor path and a redacted live Discord canary proof. The canary verified the bot identity with Discord, posted a canary message to the configured target, added a reaction through Discord REST, then exercised the PR-head reaction listener and captured the enqueue/wake result with hashed identifiers only.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output from the live Discord proof script and focused local verification:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

Supplemental verification: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts` passed locally with 1 file and 46 tests.
- Observed result after fix: The live Discord canary proved the external API path was usable, and the PR-head reaction listener queued the accepted reaction event, emitted a stable `reaction_key`, downgraded sender trust, and requested the immediate `discord-reaction` heartbeat for the routed session. Duplicate/deduped enqueue remains covered by focused regression tests and does not wake.
- What was not tested: No long-running production Discord gateway restart was performed; this proof combines a live Discord API canary with the exact PR-head listener execution and focused regression tests.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source inspection showed Discord reaction listeners enqueued system events without a heartbeat wake.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction listener code enqueued system events and returned without requesting a heartbeat wake.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted reaction enqueue/routing behavior but did not assert wake behavior for reaction-only inputs.
- Contributing context (if known): reaction event text included a formatted emoji label but not a stable key that preference text can target consistently.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reaction notifications enqueue stable reaction-key events and wake only when enqueue succeeds.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the Discord monitor harness already covers reaction notification auth, routing, and enqueue behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing reaction tests covered notification enqueue/routing but not heartbeat wake.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls now wake the routed session. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preferences/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without channel-specific semantics config.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Discord reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed only after later wake]

After:
[Discord reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor unit harness
- Relevant config (redacted): default DM reaction test config and guild `reactionNotifications` cases

### Steps

1. Run the focused Discord monitor tests.
2. Run extension typecheck.
3. Run format check for touched files.

### Expected

- Reaction event tests pass, including heartbeat wake assertions.
- Typecheck and format checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: standard emoji enqueue + wake, custom emoji stable key enqueue + wake, no wake when enqueue deduplicates, existing reaction authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked, `off`/`own`/`all`/`allowlist` modes still gate behavior, custom emoji context keys are stable.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord Gateway delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications can now start an agent turn where they previously waited for another wake.
  - Mitigation: behavior is still gated by existing `reactionNotifications` and Discord sender/channel/guild access controls, and duplicate queue entries do not wake.
target.author: 100menotu001

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
